title: ≡ƒº¬ Add Jest tests for formatting utilities
body:
## Problem
No tests exist for formatting utilities, risking incorrect display of currency, dates, and other values.

## Context
Formatting errors could cause users to see wrong prices or dates.

## Proposed Solution
Create tests for all formatting functions:
1. `formatCurrency` - various currencies, decimals
2. `formatRelativeDate` - past, future, edge cases
3. `formatBillingCycle` - all cycle types
4. `formatCategory` - all category types

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All exported functions tested
- [ ] Edge cases covered (zero, negative, null)
- [ ] Different locale considerations
- [ ] All tests pass

## Technical Notes
**File:** `src/utils/__tests__/formatting.test.ts` (new)
**Current:** `src/utils/formatting.ts`

## Constraints
- Test both positive and negative scenarios
- Test boundary conditions

title: ≡ƒöº Remove unsafe any types from API details
body:
## Problem
details?: any in api.ts loses type safety for API responses.

## Context
API responses are parsed without type checking, risking runtime errors.

## Proposed Solution
Define proper API types:
1. Create response interfaces for each endpoint
2. Replace any with specific types
3. Add JSON schema validation
4. Consider using zod for runtime validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All API types properly defined
- [ ] Response parsing type-safe
- [ ] IDE autocomplete for API data
- [ ] Build passes without errors

## Technical Notes
**File:** src/types/api.ts:49
**Type:** SubscriptionAPIResponse

## Constraints
- Handle nullable fields
- Version API responses if needed
- Document unknown fields

title: ≡ƒöº Remove unsafe type casts from HomeScreen
body:
## Problem
Multiple as never casts bypass TypeScript safety in HomeScreen.

## Context
Type casts are code smells indicating type design problems.

## Proposed Solution
Fix type issues properly:
1. Identify all as never usages
2. Determine correct type for each
3. Update type definitions if needed
4. Remove unnecessary casts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All as never casts removed
- [ ] Proper types defined
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode passes
- [ ] Runtime behavior unchanged

## Technical Notes
**Files:** 
- src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx
- src/screens/WalletConnectScreen.tsx

## Constraints
- Some casts may be necessary
- Document why if kept
- Test thoroughly after changes

title: ≡ƒöì Add proper Ethereum address validation
body:
## Problem
Address validation only checks length, not format or checksum.

## Context
Invalid addresses can be entered, leading to failed transactions.

## Proposed Solution
Implement proper validation:
1. Check for 0x prefix
2. Validate hex characters
3. Verify checksum (EIP-55 for mainnet)
4. Show error for invalid addresses

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Valid addresses accepted
- [ ] Invalid format rejected with message
- [ ] Checksum validation for mainnet
- [ ] Test with various address formats

## Technical Notes
**File:** src/screens/CryptoPaymentScreen.tsx:107
**Functions:** Add address validation utility

## Constraints
- Must handle testnet addresses
- Consider mixed case addresses
- Performance for real-time validation
